text.title: Rice For One Person Cheatsheet Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Rice For One Person Cheatsheet. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Reach = absolute affected users (80 is signal, not noise).
rule.rationale: Reach = absolute affected users (80 is signal, not noise).

rule.statement: Effort in dev-days for one person, never dev-weeks for a team.
rule.rationale: Effort in dev-days for one person, never dev-weeks for a team.

rule.statement: Confidence has exactly 3 levels: high=0.9, medium=0.7, low=0.5; no in-betweens.
rule.rationale: Confidence has exactly 3 levels: high=0.9, medium=0.7, low=0.5; no in-betweens.

rule.statement: Sunday ritual picks ONLY the #1 bet for the week; rest are queued.
rule.rationale: Sunday ritual picks ONLY the #1 bet for the week; rest are queued.

rule.statement: Re-run the ritual every Sunday; carryovers are visible.
rule.rationale: Re-run the ritual every Sunday; carryovers are visible.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-rice-for-one-person-cheatsheet.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
